content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 17, 2017

Charles A. Newman
124 Pomar Lane
Montecito, CA 93108

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-17-071

Dear Mr. Newman:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090.

QUESTION

May you participate in decisions involving the Montecito YMCA even though you are a paying member of that organization?

CONCLUSION

Yes. Since you do not have a financial interest that will be potentially affected, you do not have a disqualifying conflict of interest and may participate in and make decisions concerning the Montecito YMCA.

FACTS

A branch of the Channel Islands YMCA, located in Montecito (the "Montecito YMCA") has applied to the County of Santa Barbara for a revised conditional use permit to update its Master Plan. The proposed revisions are to modify the Montecito YMCA's existing facilities, all of which are located in Montecito and are within the Montecito Planning Commission's jurisdiction.

The Montecito YMCA is a nonprofit organization the provides recreational services to its members. Members pay monthly dues for use of facilities in Montecito and are entitled to use other

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-17-071
Page No. 2

related YMCA facilities, but do not receive any interest in the club's real property or any other
interest in the YMCA. In addition, memberships are not transferable and cannot be sold by the
member.

Membership fees for the Montecito YMCA range from $36 to $113 per month. You pay
approximately $100 per month. You are a member of the Channel Islands YMCA and use its
Montecito facility. You are not now, and have never been, an employee, officer, or director of the
Channel Islands or Montecito YMCA. You participate, as a member, in some of the group exercise
programs and visit the facility on your own. The Montecito YMCA is located more than 500 feet
from any real property in which you have an interest.

The Montecito YMCA is seeking to revise its existing conditional use permit ("Permit") to
update its Master Plan. The revisions are to renovate, enhance, and expand existing facilities and
include demolition and reconstruction of existing buildings, construction of new buildings, and
continuation and expansion of recreation, fitness, and well-being programs. The proposed facility
upgrades will include increasing interior space, of the facility, adding new indoor sports courts, a
new indoor gymnasium, new indoor locker rooms, new indoor exercise pool, expansion of the
existing outdoor pool and a new pool deck, new playgrounds, and a new café/snack bar. According
to information provided by the Montecito YMCA Executive Director, capital expansion projects are
typically funded by donations, and are not started until they are fully funded. As such, membership
fees would not need to be increased in order to fund the proposed expansion.

ANALYSIS

Conflicts of Interest under the Act

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest. The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions only apply to conflicts of
interests based on the following:

• Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth $2,000
or more. (Section 87103(a).)

• Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 or
more. (Section 87103(b).)

• Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the
regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status,
aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).)

• Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee,
or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).)

File No. A-17-071
Page No. 3

• Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating $470 or more in value provided to, received by, or promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(e).)

• A public official's own personal finances, or those of a member of his or her immediate family. (Section 87103.)

Merely being a paying member of the Montecito YMCA does not result in you having a financial interest in the Montecito YMCA. Moreover, while an official always has an interest in his or her personal finances and those of the official's immediate family members, your facts do not suggest that you will experience any measurable financial benefit or loss from the decision. Accordingly, you may participate in and make decisions concerning the Montecito YMCA.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: Zachary W. Norton
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

ZWN:jgl
